stem: A car travels at a constant speed of 45 miles per hour on a highway. Given that 1 mile = 5,280 feet and 1 hour = 60 minutes, what is the car's speed in feet per minute?
explanation:
To convert 45 miles per hour to feet per minute, apply two conversion factors:

Step 1: Convert miles to feet.
$$45 \frac{\text{mi}}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{5{,}280 \text{ ft}}{1 \text{ mi}} = 237{,}600 \frac{\text{ft}}{\text{hr}}$$

Step 2: Convert hours to minutes.
$$237{,}600 \frac{\text{ft}}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1 \text{ hr}}{60 \text{ min}} = 3{,}960 \frac{\text{ft}}{\text{min}}$$

The car travels at **3,960 feet per minute**.

distractorRationale.A: Choice A (66) results from dividing by 3,600 instead of 60 — converting to feet per *second* rather than feet per *minute*: $45 \times 5{,}280 \div 3{,}600 = 66$. The student applies both 60 seconds/minute and 60 minutes/hour as divisors, effectively converting to ft/sec.
distractorRationale.B: Choice B (2,640) results from using an incorrect conversion factor of 1 mile = 3,520 feet instead of 5,280 feet: $45 \times 3{,}520 \div 60 = 2{,}640$. A student who misremembers the feet-per-mile conversion arrives at this answer.
distractorRationale.C: Choice C (3,960) is the correct answer.
distractorRationale.D: Choice D (7,920) results from dividing by 30 instead of 60, as if there were only 30 minutes in an hour: $45 \times 5{,}280 \div 30 = 7{,}920$. The student uses half the correct divisor, doubling the result.
